sentenceFrame: The owl is in the town.
celebration: OWL DEBUTS — decodable at debut (ow + l), the first cast member the class can read the very day they meet. The ou/ow diphthong leaf goes up: the 'ouch!' sound, two spellings.

songs.title: Ow-Ow-Owl!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(ow… ow… ow…)
The 'ouch!' sound!

[Hook]
Ow! Ow! Ow-ow-ow!
ou or ow — ONE sound!
Ow-ow-owl! Ow-ow-owl!
It's an owl! (hello, Owl!)

[Verse 2]
Ow! Ow! Ow-ow-ow!
A cow! A cloud!
Out of the house!
Down in the town! (the town!)

[Verse 3]
Ow! Ow! Ow-ow-ow!
Loud! Round! Proud!
A mouse in the house!
(ow! ow! ow!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not ow!
(ow… ow… ow…)

[Outro]
ou — house… ow — owl… (ow… ow…)
Ouch! (an owl!)
(ow… shhh…)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: ou/ow here is the DIPHTHONG /aʊ/ — the 'ouch!' sound (NOT the long-o ow of Week 45 snow). It glides from 'ah' to 'oo' in one breath — ride it down like a slide. 'ow' sung as the diphthong, never letter names. Verse 4 welcomes cast member Owl (ow-l, decodable at debut). Potato bridge intact.

songs.title: The Owl in Town
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
The owl is in the town!
The owl is in the town!
Where is the owl? (where? where?)
Out and about!

[Verse 2]
The owl looks down.
A loud, loud sound!
A cow! A cow! (moo! moo!)
Out of the house!

[Verse 3]
The owl and the cow shout!
Down in the town, so proud!
Where is the owl? (where? where?)
Up! Up! It can fly!

[Outro]
The town… the owl… (shhh…)
What is in the town?!
(POTATO!)
The owl in town. (goodnight, town.)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'The owl is in the town.' — Owl's debut adventure, a night in town with a loud cow. Every content word rides the /aʊ/ glide (owl, town, down, out, loud, sound, cow, shout, proud). Let the choir hoot the owl and moo the cow. Whisper the outro; the potato is in town too.

spreads.artNote: CAST DEBUT: Owl — a small round tawny owl with huge amber eyes — blinking in the spotlight. And the class can READ its name the day it arrives: ow-l.

spreads.text: The owl is in the town.
spreads.artNote: Owl perched on a lamppost over a little sleeping town, big eyes glowing.
Owl peering straight down from the lamppost, huge eyes fixed on something below in the street.
Owl startled, feathers up — a big cartoon 'sound' burst rising from a barn at the edge of town.
The source: a cow standing in the street, looking up sheepishly. Owl stares. Comedy of the odd pair.

spreads.text: The cow is out! The cow is in the house!
spreads.artNote: The cow has wandered right into a little house, filling the doorway, a lamp knocked askew. Chaos, gentle.

spreads.text: The owl and the cow shout.
spreads.artNote: Owl hooting, cow mooing, both wide-mouthed under the moon — a duet of loud.

spreads.text: Down in the town, the owl is proud.
spreads.artNote: Owl back on the lamppost, chest puffed, having herded the cow home. Star twinkles above, Sheep waves from a window.
In the middle of the town square sits a single potato under a tiny streetlamp. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it. Owl swivels its head all the way round to look.

sentences: The owl is in the town.
The owl and the cow shout.
extras: ONE SOUND, TWO SPELLINGS (again): ou vs ow. The gentle pattern the children discover — ow tends to live at the END of a word or syllable (cow, owl, down, town), ou tends to live in the MIDDLE (out, house, loud, round). Not a hard rule, a helpful hunch. Sort a stack and let them find it.

🚨 The ow TRAP: ow already appeared in Week 45 as long-o (snow, grow). Today it is the /aʊ/ diphthong (cow, owl). Same two letters, two totally different sounds — hold up snow and cow together and let the collision be the lesson.
Pattern Tree: the class reads out/house/cow/owl aloud and hangs the ou/ow diphthong leaf. Owl's name goes up beside it — a friend read on debut day.
Moveable alphabet: build ou-t, h-ou-se, c-ow, ow-l, t-ow-n, cl-ou-d.

teacherFocus: ou/ow is the 'ouch!' diphthong /aʊ/ — a glide, not a held vowel. Slide it: 'ah' rolling into 'oo' in one breath. Teach the gentle placement hunch (ow at word/syllable ends — cow, owl, down; ou in the middle — out, house, loud) as a helper, never a hard rule. 🚨 And name the ow TRAP head-on: ow was long-o in Week 45 (snow) and is /aʊ/ now (cow) — hold snow and cow up together so the two-sounds-one-spelling collision is a discovery, not a stumble. Owl debuts and is readable on arrival — the class meets a friend and spells its name the same minute.
